methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
FleetZero is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Steven Henderson and Mike Carter that has raised $43M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 8 private transportation companies including Rivian, REGENT Craft, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger; no public comparables exist in the immediate frontier-tech transportation segment.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to FleetZero's $43M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progression in capital-intensive sectors. This yields a midpoint valuation of $86M. The peer-median method could not be applied: while 5 transportation peers exist in the dataset, none yielded three or more datapoints when filtered for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability. Relying on the broader all-sector median would introduce material bias given the capital and regulatory intensity distinctive to transportation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $73M (low) to $99M (high), with $86M as the central estimate. This range is anchored exclusively to stage-typical progression and reflects a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable Series A transportation transactions and the early stage at which meaningful unit economics or go-to-market traction typically remain unvalidated. Investors should expect material variance depending on FleetZero's demonstrated progress on capital efficiency and customer acquisition since the most recent financing.
